column3: Definition
A business model where individual locations operate under a unified brand with standardized processes.
Ensuring all franchise locations follow corporate-mandated processes, branding, and reporting requirements.
column4: “I’ve been publishing once a month the compliance report. Through the month of January, we were 66% compliant. Some are still using QuickBooks, some are on paper, and the rest are in between.”
column7: “If they’re creating the sales transaction in QuickBooks or creating it in Method like we want them to do, we can measure those things.”

column3: Guidelines that franchisees must adhere to for maintaining consistency across all locations.
The ability to track financials and sales across multiple independent franchise locations.
column4: “What the goal was is I need a report that shows me the exact things people are selling. In the old world in QuickBooks, people have the ability to call those things whatever they want.”

column3: A single point-of-sale platform used across all locations to standardize transactions.
The rate at which franchisees implement and use the mandated technology or processes.
The system used to process sales transactions and accept payments in-store.
Enforcing uniform product naming conventions in transactions across franchise locations.
The tracking of product stock levels across multiple locations.
column4:
Craig Kreakie:
"What the goal was is I need a report that shows me the exact things people are selling. In the old world in QuickBooks, people have the ability to call those things whatever they want. So, if I have this blue pen, pretend Store A calls this 'giraffe' and Store B calls this 'elephant.' So when I try to report in the reports, they appear as two different things, but in reality, they’re one."
column5: By forcing franchisees to use a standardized inventory system in Method CRM, the company gains full visibility into stock movement across all locations.
column6:
Craig Kreakie:
"So what we did was we standardized the items within Method, and then now they have to use the Method sales receipt. So we can control the cash register, meaning we’re controlling the item numbers that they’re selling so that we have reports that we can now share, use ourselves, but also share with them on how they’re doing."

column3: A unique identifier for each product to standardize inventory tracking.
The structured process of moving a customer purchase from order to payment to recordkeeping.
A third-party service that processes credit card transactions for retail businesses.
The ability to track sales trends and customer purchases across multiple locations. Without a centralized view of product sales, businesses cannot track sales performance across locations.

column3: Organizing sales data by broader product categories instead of individual SKUs.

column6: Custom workflows were built in Method CRM to capture the necessary data for reporting and standardization.

column4:
Craig Kreakie:
"As a franchisee, they have an obligation to report their revenues, but the next question was: okay, what’s the details of their revenues? There’s no answers because everyone is running their own financials and running their own transactions."
column5: Lack of centralized revenue tracking makes it difficult to analyze sales trends, negotiate supplier pricing, or forecast growth in multi-location businesses.
column6: Method standardizes sales tracking, ensuring franchise-wide revenue visibility.

Features designed to prevent franchisees from bypassing required workflows in the CRM.
Tailor-made interfaces and processes in a CRM to meet specific business needs.
Adapting CRM software to fit the needs of a franchise or multi-location business.
Franchisees preferring older systems (QuickBooks, paper-based processes) over new technology.
column4:
Craig Kreakie:
"When I came on board a year ago, my boss said, 'We are not changing again. You have to use Method.' And right away, I didn’t like Method because I didn’t like the way it was originally designed. But at that time, I didn’t realize how customizable it was."

column3: The franchisor’s right to mandate technology adoption per franchise agreements.
